It belongs to the genus Limniris, which is a group of irises native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. I couldn't find any information on the plant Limniris shrevei. However, based on the name, it might be a species of the genus Limniris, which is a synonym for the genus Lilium. The plant Lilium, commonly known as the lily, is a flowering plant with the following characteristics:

Lilium is a genus of flowering plants in the family Liliaceae, characterized by showy, funnel-shaped flowers and narrow, strap-like leaves. The health benefits of Lilium include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lilium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lilium include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat colds, fever, and respiratory issues.
